title: Panamax market
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas
This time last year, seasonal optimism proved elusive for the Panamax segment. The Baltic Dry Index closed at 1,537 points, a level at odds with the bullish sentiment that traditionally accompanies the onset of winter grain and coal demand. Rather than a surge in fixing activity and rising rates, the market drifted downward, seemingly searching for a floor.
